content.claim: Around 85% of Hungarian students attend schools with the ability to select students based on academic performance or teacher recommendations, compared to an OECD average of 43%
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Figure captures broad selective-admission mechanisms (including 4-year gimnaziums and vocational tracks), not solely 6- and 8-year extended tracks
Does not establish causal impact of selection on student outcomes
Cross-national comparison may reflect different institutional definitions of 'selection'
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
